figures.caption: Table 7: Performance comparison of various methods on the AVUT [29] benchmark.
Table 6: Performance comparison of various methods on the WorldSense [13] benchmark.
Table 5: Comparison of OmniZip, DASH, and OmniDrop.
Figure 1: (a) PCA visualization of audio and video token distributions in the LLM embedding space. (b) Distribution of cosine similarities among audio-audio, video-video and audio-video token pairs.

Table 2: Comprehensive performance and efficiency comparison of different methods on Qwen2.5Omni models. Avg. ∆Perf. denotes the average performance change across four benchmarks when reducing the retained ratio from 30% to 20%.

Figure 2: (a) Layer-wise text-to-audiovisual attention scores, averaged within each audiovisual chunk and normalized for better visualization. We show representative decoder layers, i.e., Layers 3, 14, and 22. The first and second rows correspond to the queries “Who said in the video that ‘...’?” and “In which part of the video does the man with ... appear?”, respectively. (b) Top-20% attention recall of text-to-audio and text-to-video attention scores across decoder layers.

Figure 4: Comparison of audio/video retained ratio per decoder-layer across three different tasks, Audio Source Localization (ASL), Scene Recognition (SR), and Audio Recognition (AR). (a) Audio retained ratios across tasks. (b) Video retained ratio across tasks. Our query-guided pruning enables retention ratios adapted to task requirements without explicit task labels.

Figure 3: Overview of OmniDrop. (a) Progressive layer-wise token pruning (PLP) schedule based on the sigmoid function (Eqs. (3) and (4)). (b) Query-guided token importance (Eq. 5) with a temporal diversity score (Alg. 1) for audiovisual tokens at each layer.

Figure 5: (a) Layer-wise text-to-audiovisual attention scores, averaged within each audiovisual chunk and normalized for better visualization. We show representative decoder layers, i.e., Layers 9, 19, and 31. The first and second rows correspond to the queries “Who said in the video that ‘...’?” and “In which part of the video does the man with ... appear?”, respectively. (b) Top-20% attention recall of text-to-audio and text-to-video attention scores across decoder layers.
